list with tmux absent (no server or exitCode 1) -> empty-friendly message exit 0
rm refuses non-jeo- name with exitCode 1
rm jeo- name invokes kill-session argv
attach unknown name (not in list output) -> exitCode 1
attach known name -> invokes attach -t name
unknown verb usage exitCode 1
--help usage exitCode 0
list keeps marker-owned sessions regardless of name; records branch/project identity
list table shows the @jeo-branch identity column
rm allows a marker-owned session even without the jeo- prefix (exact kill target)
rm still refuses an unmarked, unprefixed session even when it exists
exportSession: markdown includes header + non-system messages by default
exportSession: includeSystem keeps the system message
exportSession: json is structured and parseable
exportSession: tolerates a malformed trailing line
exportSession: markdown fence is longer than backtick runs in the content
session lifecycle and logic with custom cwd
session default cwd behavior
session rename and delete lifecycle
session resume with compaction marker and legacy compatibility
listSessions uses lightweight parser (avoids full JSON.parse)
normalizeBaseUrl defaults, adds scheme, strips trailing slash
chooseDefaultModel: blank → provider's recommended
chooseDefaultModel: known id accepted; provider mismatch warns
chooseDefaultModel: unknown id accepted with suggestions
recommendedModelsFor returns annotated id lines
recommendedModelsFor can show Codex OAuth OpenAI defaults
buildEnabledProviders detects keys, oauth, and base URLs
buildSetupSummary reports the resolved provider + catalog metadata
workflowSkillsForPrompt ignores a user doc named 'team' with a foreign summary
buildSkillTask clamps long details and contains the anti-recite line
looksLikeSkillEcho detects replies dominated by skill-doc content
buildSkillChoices shows aliases as hints
skillPicker renders a keyboard-selectable skill list
skills command: list, read, legacy alias, json output, unknown skill handling
parseSkillMarkdown infers summary from the first body line; strips a title
parseSkillMarkdown honors explicit header keys
parseSkillMarkdown extracts explicit and inferred slash aliases
parseSkillMarkdown ignores inferred slash aliases owned by other skills
parseSkillMarkdown infers aliases for the last segment of a namespaced skill name
parseSkillMarkdown skips YAML frontmatter and uses folded description as summary
loadSkills merges bundled + user skill docs; user overrides by name
loadSkills skips hidden system dirs and external skills that collide with builtin command names
parseSkillMarkdown round-trips the formatSkill (jeo skills --write) decoration
loadSkills loads skills from JEO_SKILLS_DIR (positive path)
loadSkills overrides bundled skills case-insensitively by filename
(unnamed)
buildSkillTask: drives execution with real tools, not a skill-named tool call
buildSkillTask: no intent → still instructs the agent to carry out the workflow
parseSkillMarkdown folds YAML block scalars and the lead-in '... >' form
parseSkillMarkdown caps oversized details to keep skill invocation prompts bounded
skillNames returns all six bundled skills
getSkill case-insensitive lookup
getSkill returns undefined for non-existent skill
formatSkill output format
skillsPromptSection contains every skill name
skillsPromptSection caps an oversized skill catalog and reports omitted entries
workflowSkillsForPrompt advertises user skills but blocks reserved-name override (hijack guard)
skillsPromptSection includes configured skills and respects budget limits
parseSkillInvocation only matches explicit slash invocations
parseSkillInvocation can load an explicit skill file path but still blocks reserved meta-skill names
parseSkillInvocation: direct file path invocation (without /skill: prefix)
runSkillsCommand --write: materializes one .md per skill
SKILLS is derived from SKILL.md embeds
skillInvocationCard: name + tree-connector path/prompt detail; bundled skills labeled
matchSlash: prefix matches lead, fuzzy subsequence hits trail (case-insensitive)
isSlashAttempt: slash without a space
SLASH_COMMANDS includes the config and role-model commands
slash command details stay in sync with command names
matchSlash exposes /model and /fast without the removed /models menu item
SLASH_COMMANDS includes the code-view commands (view/diff/find/search)
matchSlash resolves the code-view command prefixes
formatSlashCommandList lists all commands for bare slash and narrows by prefix
formatSlashCommandList returns an unknown hint for non-matches
formatSlashPreview: live preview for a slash keyword prefix
formatSlashPreview: empty for non-slash, argument input, or no match
formatSlashPreview: overflow scrolls a window within the row budget with ↑/↓ markers
formatSlashPreview: a far-down selection stays visible inside the scroll window
formatSlashPreview: selected index marks the highlighted row with ▸
slashPreviewMatches: command names in display order; empty for args/non-slash
dynamic skill slash aliases appear in palette, preview, and selection order
formatSlashPreview: overflow shows an (i/total) position counter that tracks selected
$ prefix pops up matching skills with summaries (live popup)
slashPreviewMatches returns $skill names for arrow-key navigation
activeTriggerToken: finds /cmd and $skill tokens anywhere in the line
formatSlashPreview: command popup opens for a /token at any position
formatSlashPreview: skill popup opens for a $token at any position
slashPreviewMatches: arrow-selection matches follow the mid-text token
tabCompleteSelection: highlighted row wins, else top match; trailing space closes the popup
version prints
autopilot loop ratchets to best and converges
frozen eval is immutable without --force
step reverts a regression and keeps an improvement
gate goal keys on exit code
ledger reaches verified verdict
ledger rejects bad review status
